topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for bunching designs when the running variable is observed only through deterministic rounding or heaping.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp partial-identification frontier for bunching excess mass when the latent running variable is observed only through a known deterministic rounding or heaping map.

source_refs.claim: Develops point and partial identification for general bunching designs; point identification relies on analyticity of the counterfactual density.
source_refs.author: Myunghyun Song

source_refs.claim: Shows that a kink does not identify elasticity under a fully flexible heterogeneity distribution and proposes weaker nonparametric and semiparametric assumptions.
source_refs.author: Marinho Bertanha, Andrew H. McCallum, Nathan Seegert

source_refs.claim: Shows standard RD estimation with a rounded running variable can be inconsistent even when the functional form is correct.
source_refs.author: Yingying Dong

source_refs.claim: Provides partial-identification bounds for regressions with interval data without restrictions on the unobserved values inside intervals.
source_refs.author: Charles F. Manski, Elie Tamer

source_refs.excerpt: P consists of the two cutoff-side weak limits P_- and P_+ of (Y,D,W) given the running variable
running variable is subject to bounded manipulation, with the manipulation magnitude bounded by a McCrary-style

open_problems.why_unsolved: The bunching literature identifies or bounds excess mass by imposing structure on the counterfactual density, while the rounded-running-variable and interval-data literatures show that coarsening can destroy standard RD or regression identification. The prior RDD manipulation proposal assumes observable cutoff-side weak limits of the true running variable; deterministic heaping instead exposes only cell totals, so the missing object is the sharp aliasing frontier over latent densities that produce the same rounded histogram.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to apply generic interval-data bounds fails because bunching mass is a local counterfactual-density functional and deterministic heap cells can contain signed latent-density perturbations that preserve every observed rounded count while changing excess mass.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp-bound theorem giving necessary and sufficient conditions for the lower and upper excess-mass endpoints under a known rounding map, together with a finite signed-nullspace witness certifying nonidentification when the endpoints differ.

open_problems.open_problem: Find the phase threshold in heap-grid width and excluded-window geometry at which deterministic rounding makes a bunching design informative or uninformative.

source_refs.claim: Shows heaping can bias RD estimates, with bias depending on bandwidth choice and heap location relative to the threshold, and that usual diagnostics may fail.
source_refs.author: Alan I. Barreca, Jason M. Lindo, Glen R. Waddell

source_refs.claim: Documents that bunching estimates depend crucially on the chosen bunching window and proposes a data-driven window procedure.
source_refs.author: Nicole Bosch, Egbert Jongen, Arjan Lejour

source_refs.claim: Shows donut RD estimators can have substantially larger bias and variance than conventional RD estimators.
source_refs.author: Cladia Noack, Christoph Rothe

source_refs.claim: Uses excess bunching and missing mass around notches to identify frictions and structural elasticities.
source_refs.author: Henrik J. Kleven, Mazhar Waseem

source_refs.excerpt: The open part is the passage from that exact network oracle to the unrestricted finite-support many-window problem.
computes only one fixed lambda,m slice, not the global lower/upper envelope from primitives

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published work treats heaping, bandwidth, and donut trimming as bias or robustness problems, and notch bunching exploits missing mass once the latent distribution is effectively observed. The prior many-window RDD flow attempt also failed precisely at the passage from fixed slices to a global endpoint envelope. The unresolved issue is a geometry result: which alignments of heap cells, excluded windows, and usable neighboring cells leave enough constraints to separate true bunching from aliasing.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to trim heaps or enlarge the excluded window fails because deleting aliased cells can also delete the constraints that distinguish latent bunching mass from counterfactual-density curvature.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A phase-threshold theorem stating, for each grid spacing, cutoff alignment, and excluded-window configuration, whether the sharp bound is nontrivial, plus an explicit witness at the uninformative side of the threshold.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop falsification and inference for deterministic-heaping bunching designs using finite signed-nullspace certificates rather than density-continuity or ordinary discrete-running-variable diagnostics.

source_refs.claim: Develops a density-continuity test for manipulation of the running variable in RD designs.
source_refs.author: Justin McCrary

source_refs.claim: Shows cluster-by-running-variable confidence intervals can have poor coverage in RD designs with a moderate number of support points.
source_refs.author: Michal Kolesar, Christoph Rothe

source_refs.claim: Derives conditions under which measurement error in the running variable can be ignored, including settings with discreteness or irregular support handled by bias-aware methods.
source_refs.author: Yingying Dong, Michal Kolesar

source_refs.excerpt: two feasible refinements kappa_- and kappa_+ induce the same law P(Y,C)
Reusable finite latent-timing refinement witness, but not enough by itself to support a stronger novelty claim.

open_problems.why_unsolved: RD diagnostics test density manipulation, discrete-support inference, or conditions under which measurement error can be ignored; they do not ask whether two latent score densities induce identical rounded histograms while implying different bunching mass. The banked interval-censoring proposal supplies the observational-equivalence template but only for DiD event-time refinements, not for local density and bunching functionals.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to use McCrary-style density tests fails because deterministic heaping can create or erase reported spikes under aggregation while leaving the latent signed aliasing direction invisible in the rounded histogram.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A certificate theorem and inference procedure that either constructs a feasible signed-nullspace witness changing the bunching estimand or proves no such witness exists under stated smoothness and monotonicity restrictions, with confidence sets for the resulting interval.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify the weakest shape restrictions under which rounded-score interval logic collapses to informative bunching endpoints, and give counterexamples when endpoint pruning is invalid.

source_refs.claim: Emphasizes that bunching methods rely on assumptions about an unobserved distribution and that the correct assumption cannot be determined from the data.
source_refs.author: Marinho Bertanha, Andrew H. McCallum, Alexis Payne, Nathan Seegert

source_refs.claim: Shows continuous measurement error smooths discontinuities and causes loss of RD identification unless auxiliary data or additional structure is available.
source_refs.author: Laurent Davezies, Thomas Le Barbanchon

source_refs.claim: Characterizes sharp identification regions for models with convex moment predictions, including interval covariate and outcome data, using random sets and convex programming.
source_refs.author: Arie Beresteanu, Ilya Molchanov, Francesca Molinari

source_refs.excerpt: frontier pruning dropped non-frontier lower/upper cells without an endpoint-cell stochastic-dominance
several interval-measured regressors create incomparable endpoint cells

open_problems.why_unsolved: Recent bunching papers permit partial identification after relaxing analyticity, measurement-error RD papers show that extra structure is needed to recover discontinuities, and convex/interval-data methods give general sharp-set machinery. The prior interval-regression failure records that naive frontier pruning is invalid without additional dominance. The missing result is a restriction ladder tailored to deterministic heap fibers near a bunching kink or notch.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to replace each rounded score by interval endpoints fails because endpoint dominance for generic interval regressors does not imply that latent mass inside a heap cell is irrelevant for local density or excess-mass functionals.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A hierarchy of sufficient and necessary shape restrictions, such as bounded curvature, monotone density ratios, log-concavity, or analyticity, stating exactly which signed-nullspace witnesses survive each restriction and when scalar endpoint formulas are sharp.

literature_map:
Saez (2010, AEJ Policy) and Chetty-Friedman-Olsen-Pistaferri (2011, QJE) anchor kink bunching by translating excess mass into elasticity through a smooth counterfactual density.
Kleven-Waseem (2013, QJE) add notches, where missing mass and dominated regions provide extra identifying structure.
Bertanha-McCallum-Seegert (2021, FEDS; later Journal of Econometrics version) is the key identification warning: notches identify more than kinks under flexible heterogeneity, and estimates are assumption-sensitive.
Bertanha-McCallum-Payne-Seegert (2022, Stata Journal) operationalize nonparametric and semiparametric bunching estimators and stress that assumptions on the unobserved distribution are not data-verifiable.
Song (2024, arXiv) gives recent general point and partial identification for bunching, with analyticity central for point identification and inequality restrictions for partial identification.
Bosch-Jongen-Lejour (2020, International Tax and Public Finance) and Dekker-Schweikert (2021, Public Finance Review) show bunching-window choices matter for estimates.
Barreca-Lindo-Waddell (2016, Economic Inquiry) is the closest heaping warning: usual RD diagnostics may miss non-random heaping, and bias depends on bandwidth and heap location.
Dong (2015, Journal of Applied Econometrics), Davezies-Le Barbanchon (2017, Journal of Econometrics), and Dong-Kolesar (2023, Journal of Applied Econometrics) cover rounded or mismeasured RD running variables, but not bunching-specific heap aliasing.
McCrary (2008, Journal of Econometrics), Kolesar-Rothe (2018, AER), and Noack-Rothe (2023, arXiv) cover manipulation tests, discrete-support inference, and donut trimming; none gives an aliasing frontier for rounded bunching histograms.
Manski-Tamer (2002, Econometrica) and Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari (2011, Econometrica) provide interval/coarsening partial-identification machinery, but not finite signed-nullspace witnesses for bunching mass.
The live gap is therefore not generic deconvolution or LP duality; it is the sharp relationship between deterministic heap fibers, excluded-window geometry, and local excess-mass functionals.
prior_proposal_map:
Track A inspected local doc/formalization proposal .tex files, rejected angle .tex files, reviewer JSONs/JSONLs, bank READMEs, and optional pipeline/state/gaps files.
No exact prior bunching/heaping proposal was found.
pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 supplied the nearest accepted adjacent RDD signal: it assumes cutoff-side weak limits of the true running variable and bounded manipulation, which differs from deterministic heap fibers.
pid_rdd_flow_manipulation/v1 supplied a failed many-window/global-envelope warning: a fixed-slice graph or dual certificate did not compute global lower/upper endpoints.
pid_interval_censored_adoption/v1 supplied a reusable latent-refinement witness idea, but in DiD event-time rather than local running-variable density geometry.
pid_multivariate_interval_regression/v1 supplied the endpoint-pruning warning: interval-cell frontier pruning was invalid without an endpoint-cell dominance condition.
pid_text_treatment_distortion/v1 supplied a fiber-invariance analogy, but was substantively unrelated to running variables and was not used as a final source ref.
The study-pipeline gaps were reviewed as augmenting context; the LATE and Manski/Tamer interval-inference items are mostly orthogonal, while the interval/coarsening failures support the need for source-anchored partial-identification rather than point-ID seeds.
